initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Row / column domains
% -------------------------------------------------
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cell domain
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Rectangle predicate  a full border of colour2
% -------------------------------------------------
%   rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol)  holds iff the rectangle with
%   top row T, bottom row B, left column Lc and right column Rcol
%   has *all* cells on its perimeter coloured 2.
rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol) :-
    T < B,
    Lc < Rcol,
    #count{ C : input(T, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ C : input(B, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ Rr1 : input(Rr1, Lc, 2), Rr1 >= T, Rr1 <= B } = B - T + 1,
    #count{ Rr2 : input(Rr2, Rcol, 2), Rr2 >= T, Rr2 <= B } = B - T + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells  cells that are not colour2 and lie
%  strictly inside a full rectangle.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(Rcell,Ccell) :-
    not input(Rcell,Ccell,2),
    rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol),
    Rcell > T,
    Rcell < B,
    Ccell > Lc,
    Ccell < Rcol.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output colours
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rcell,Ccell,3) :- interior(Rcell,Ccell).   % inside a rectangle  colour3
output(Rcell,Ccell,0) :- not interior(Rcell,Ccell).% outside  colour0

% Exactly one colour per cell (choose any allowed colour)
1 { output(Rcell,Ccell,Col) : Col, colour(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rcell,Ccell).

% No colour other than 0 or 3 may appear
:- output(Rcell,Ccell,X), X != 0, X != 3.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Row / column domains
% -------------------------------------------------
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cell domain
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Rectangle predicate  a full border of colour2
% -------------------------------------------------
%   rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol)  holds iff the rectangle with
%   top row T, bottom row B, left column Lc and right column Rcol
%   has *all* cells on its perimeter coloured 2.
rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol) :-
    T < B,
    Lc < Rcol,
    #count{ C : input(T, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ C : input(B, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ Rr1 : input(Rr1, Lc, 2), Rr1 >= T, Rr1 <= B } = B - T + 1,
    #count{ Rr2 : input(Rr2, Rcol, 2), Rr2 >= T, Rr2 <= B } = B - T + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells  cells that are not colour2 and lie
%  strictly inside a full rectangle.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(Rcell,Ccell) :-
    not input(Rcell,Ccell,2),
    rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol),
    Rcell > T,
    Rcell < B,
    Ccell > Lc,
    Ccell < Rcol.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output colours
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rcell,Ccell,3) :- interior(Rcell,Ccell).   % inside a rectangle  colour3
output(Rcell,Ccell,0) :- not interior(Rcell,Ccell).% outside  colour0

% Exactly one colour per cell (choose any allowed colour)
1 { output(Rcell,Ccell,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rcell,Ccell).

% No colour other than 0 or 3 may appear
:- output(Rcell,Ccell,X), X != 0, X != 3.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Row / column domains
% -------------------------------------------------
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cell domain
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Rectangle predicate  a full border of colour2
% -------------------------------------------------
%   rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol)  holds iff the rectangle with
%   top row T, bottom row B, left column Lc and right column Rcol
%   has *all* cells on its perimeter coloured 2.
rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol) :-
    T < B,
    Lc < Rcol,
    #count{ C : input(T, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ C : input(B, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ Rr1 : input(Rr1, Lc, 2), Rr1 >= T, Rr1 <= B } = B - T + 1,
    #count{ Rr2 : input(Rr2, Rcol, 2), Rr2 >= T, Rr2 <= B } = B - T + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells  cells that are not colour2 and lie
%  strictly inside a full rectangle.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(Rcell,Ccell) :-
    not input(Rcell,Ccell,2),
    rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol),
    Rcell > T,
    Rcell < B,
    Ccell > Lc,
    Ccell < Rcol.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output colours
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rcell,Ccell,3) :- interior(Rcell,Ccell).   % inside a rectangle  colour3
output(Rcell,Ccell,0) :- not interior(Rcell,Ccell).% outside  colour0

% Exactly one colour per cell (choose any allowed colour)
1 { output(Rcell,Ccell,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rcell,Ccell).

% No colour other than 0 or 3 may appear
:- output(Rcell,Ccell,X), X != 0, X != 3.

#show output/3.

final_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% -------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Row / column domains
% -------------------------------------------------
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cell domain
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Rectangle predicate  a full border of colour2
% -------------------------------------------------
%   rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol)  holds iff the rectangle with
%   top row T, bottom row B, left column Lc and right column Rcol
%   has *all* cells on its perimeter coloured 2.
rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol) :-
    T < B,
    Lc < Rcol,
    #count{ C : input(T, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ C : input(B, C, 2), C >= Lc, C <= Rcol } = Rcol - Lc + 1,
    #count{ Rr1 : input(Rr1, Lc, 2), Rr1 >= T, Rr1 <= B } = B - T + 1,
    #count{ Rr2 : input(Rr2, Rcol, 2), Rr2 >= T, Rr2 <= B } = B - T + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells  cells that are not colour2 and lie
%  strictly inside a full rectangle.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior(Rcell,Ccell) :-
    not input(Rcell,Ccell,2),
    rect(T,B,Lc,Rcol),
    Rcell > T,
    Rcell < B,
    Ccell > Lc,
    Ccell < Rcol.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output colours
% -------------------------------------------------
output(Rcell,Ccell,3) :- interior(Rcell,Ccell).   % inside a rectangle  colour3
output(Rcell,Ccell,0) :- not interior(Rcell,Ccell).% outside  colour0

% Exactly one colour per cell (choose any allowed colour)
1 { output(Rcell,Ccell,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(Rcell,Ccell).

% No colour other than 0 or 3 may appear
:- output(Rcell,Ccell,X), X != 0, X != 3.

#show output/3.
